zh.Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England, United: 英國英格蘭史洛普爾史福斯波利
zh.Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England: 英國英格蘭史洛普爾史福斯波利
zh.lifeSummary: 當William Le Childe Knight, Provost Shewsbury於1140年出生在英國英格蘭史洛普爾史福斯波利時，他的父親Sir Provost William le Childe de Torpel19歲，母親Lady Wiiliam le Child18歲。他於大約1158年與Mrs William le Childe在英國英格蘭史洛普爾史福斯波利結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他去世於1199年在他的家鄉享年59歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Le Childe Knight, Provost Shewsbury于1140年出生在英国英格兰史洛普爾史福斯波利时，他的父亲Sir Provost William le Childe de Torpel19岁，母亲Lady Wiiliam le Child18岁。他于大约1158年与Mrs William le Childe在英国英格兰史洛普爾史福斯波利结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。他去世于1199年在他的家乡享年59岁。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Childe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Child
meanings: nickname from Middle English child ‘child, infant’ (Old English cild), in various possible applications. The word is found in Old English as a byname, and in Middle English as a widely used affectionate term of address. It was also used as a term of status for a young man of noble birth, although the exact meaning is not clear; in the 13th and 14th centuries it was a technical term used of a young noble awaiting elevation to the knighthood. In other cases it may have been applied as a byname to a youth considerably younger than his brothers or to one who was a minor on the death of his father.
in Kent, possibly a topographic name from Old English cielde ‘spring (water)’, a rare word derived from c(e)ald ‘cold’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Le Childe Knight, Provost Shewsbury was born in 1140, in Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Provost William le Childe de Torpel, was 19 and his mother, Lady Wiiliam le Child, was 18. He married Mrs William le Childe about 1158, in Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He died in 1199, in his hometown, at the age of 59.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Le Childe Knight, Provost Shewsbury est né en 1140, à Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Provost William le Childe de Torpel, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Lady Wiiliam le Child, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Mrs William le Childe environ 1158, à Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Il est décédé en 1199, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 59 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Le Childe Knight, Provost Shewsbury 1140 in Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Provost William le Childe de Torpel 19 und seine Mutter Lady Wiiliam le Child 18. Er hat um 1158 in Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom Mrs William le Childe geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Er starb 1199 im Alter von 59 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Le Childe Knight, Provost Shewsbury è nato il nel 1140 nel Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir Provost William le Childe de Torpel, aveva 19 anni e il suo madre, Lady Wiiliam le Child, aveva 18 anni. Lui sposato Mrs William le Childe circa 1158, nel Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì nel 1199, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 59 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: William Le Childe Knight, Provost Shewsbury(이)가 1140년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 슈레w스버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Provost William le Childe de Torpel(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Wiiliam le Child(은)는 18세였다. 그는 대략 1158년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 슈레w스버이에서 Mrs William le Childe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1199년 그의 고향에서 59세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Le Childe Knight, Provost Shewsbury nasceu em 1140, em Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Provost William le Childe de Torpel, tinha 19 anos e sua mãe, Lady Wiiliam le Child, tinha 18 anos. Ele casou-se com Mrs William le Childe aproximadamente 1158, em Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1199, em sua cidade natal, com 59 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Le Childe Knight, Provost Shewsbury родился в 1140 г. в местности шрюсбури, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Provost William le Childe de Torpel было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Wiiliam le Child, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs William le Childe около 1158 г. в местности шрюсбури, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Он умер в 1199 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 59 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Le Childe Knight, Provost Shewsbury nació en 1140, en Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Provost William le Childe de Torpel, tenía 19 años y su mamá, Lady Wiiliam le Child, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Mrs William le Childe aproximadamente 1158, en Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió en 1199, en su pueblo, a la edad de 59 años.
